ℹ️ How to read
📊 How to use this section
Step 1:The donut chart shows three top-level cause groups at a glance
Step 2:Click a group button to drill into specific causes within that category
Human:12 specific causes ranked by fire count — debris burning, arson, equipment etc.
Lightning:Top 15 states by lightning fire count — the only natural cause in U.S. data
Unknown:Missing or undefined cause records — 8.9% of all fires
⚡
Lightning is the only natural ignition source in U.S. wildfire records. Lightning fires tend to be larger — they ignite in remote areas with limited suppression access.

Pure risk premium — top 15 states
Actuarial: avg fires/yr × avg fire size
ℹ️ What is this?
📊 Pure Risk Premium — Explained
Formula:Avg fires per year × avg acres per fire
Meaning:Expected annual acres burned — the actuarial foundation of wildfire insurance pricing
Idaho:High severity-driven risk — fewer fires but each burns hundreds of acres on average
California:High frequency-driven risk — nearly 8,000 fires per year but smaller average size
Key insight:Two states can have similar pure premiums through completely opposite risk profiles — insurers must price them differently

U.S. Wildfire Risk Explorer · Data: FPA FOD Database (USFS/USGS) · Analysis: Princess Tagoe
1,880,465 records · 52 states/territories · 1992–2015 · Maps use D3.js + US Atlas topology
